schema_notes: Single-subject per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Tier classification happens downstream.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual research run — Len Blavatnik + regen_v3@sha256-ca0b4f1d66efde50 + regen_v3@sha256-fba267158d656d01 + regen_v3@sha256-c2620f2fab593204

person.citizenship_notes: Born Odessa, Ukrainian SSR (Soviet Union), June 14, 1957. Family emigrated to the US in 1978. Naturalized US citizen; naturalized UK citizen; also holds Greek citizenship. Knighted by Queen Elizabeth II in 2017 in the Birthday Honours 'for services to philanthropy.' Chevalier of the French Legion d'Honneur (2013).
person.wealth_source: Access Industries (founded 1986). Fortune built through post-Soviet aluminum (Sual/Rusal) and oil (Tyumen Oil / TNK-BP joint venture with Viktor Vekselberg and Mikhail Fridman's Alfa Group; sold 50% stake to Rosneft in 2013 for ~$28B gross, ~$7B personal). Now chairman of Warner Music Group (acquired 2011), ~21% stake in LyondellBasell, owner of DAZN, significant real estate and venture portfolio.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires March 2026 ~$29.9B, rank ~#78. Forbes April 2025 snapshot ~$26.5B (rank #75). Celebrity Net Worth (lower-quality source) ~$40B. We use $29.9B as best estimate; $26.5-30.4B is the credible Forbes band. Bloomberg Billionaires Index tracks a similar range.

sources.fetch_note: Forbes profile page was rate-limited via WebFetch; value triangulated from Forbes search result snippets and Wikipedia citing Forbes.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Access Industries is a private holding company. Warner Music Group (WMG) is public — Blavatnik/Access holds majority voting stake, marked liquid but with control-premium drag on mark-to-market. LyondellBasell stake (~21%) is public and more readily divestible. DAZN, real estate, and venture portfolio are private and illiquid. Apply ~45% liquidity for giving-capacity-now. The 2013 TNK-BP sale produced ~$7B cash personally (documented), of which a substantial portion has since been redeployed — so cash reserves are not negligible but not fully visible.

cited_events.recipient: Multiple (108 awards in 2024; recipients not parsed to line-item here)

cited_events.notes: 2020 grants spiked — likely COVID-era giving plus the Harvard $200M 2018 pledge disbursement schedule.
2019 was the highest annual payout observed — likely coinciding with Harvard $200M multi-year disbursement.

Second Blavatnik foundation entity established 2020 (tax-exempt 2021). Trustees include Alexander Blavatnik (Len's brother) and Sigmund D Heller. Appears to be a parallel grantmaking vehicle, possibly used to route Harvard/Yale/Tel Aviv pass-through gifts separate from BFF. Dedup risk — see dedup_notes section.

First filing year for this entity. Combined with BFF (81-2444350) 2021 total of $36.5M, the family's 2021 grantmaking across these two vehicles was ~$149.6M. 2020 entity appears to have received a large upfront transfer in 2021 and disbursed most of it in 2021-2022.

cited_events.donor_entity: Warner Music Group Blavatnik Family Foundation Social Justice Fund
cited_events.notes: Joint WMG/Blavatnik vehicle launched June 2020 with $100M 10-year pledge for social justice. Actual disbursements modest (~$3.6M over 5 years). Separately funded by WMG, not purely Blavatnik — attribution to him is partial.

cited_events.source_quote: The Blavatnik Family Foundation... Over the past decade, it has contributed more than $700 million to 250+ institutions worldwide.
cited_events.alternate_source_quote: Over the past decade, the Blavatnik Family Foundation has contributed more than $1 billion to over 250 organizations.
cited_events.classification_note: Self-reported. Our observable sum from BFF (81-2444350) 2017-2024 alone is ~$556M, plus BFF-2020 (85-1345780) 2021-2022 is ~$175.8M. Combined ~$732M in the 2017-2024 window. Consistent with the foundation's own $700M-$1B claim once pre-2017 UK gifts (Oxford £75M, Tate £50M, Harvard 2013 $50M, Yale 2013 $10M) are included. No large gap between claim and evidence.

Attribution to Blavatnik personally vs. WMG corporate is mixed.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: OpenSecrets/Bellingcat triangulation: Blavatnik, his wife, and Access Industries have contributed >$10M in state + federal political contributions (~$9.2M federal, ~$1.25M state) across 2015-2022 cycles. $6.35M concentrated 2015-2017 to Republican entities; $1M direct to Trump 2017 inauguration via Access Industries; $383K to RNC 2016; additional donations across cycles to Schumer, Buttigieg, Harris, Clinton via Emily Blavatnik. FEC bulk pull for 2024/2026 cycles NOT yet executed.

sources.fetch_note: Direct WebFetch returned 403; aggregated figures drawn from OpenSecrets-cited press below.

sources.note: $1M to Trump 2017 inauguration via Access Industries; $383K to RNC from April 2016.
$10M+ aggregate across Blavatnik, wife, and Access Industries by 2022.

foundations_active.name: Blavatnik Family Foundation
foundations_active.established_year_note: Tax-exempt determination September 2018; EIN suggests formation ~2016-2017.
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (trustee listed as 'CPTC LLC' with no reported compensation).
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants ($44.6M) vs assets ($57.5M) = 77% — well above 5% minimum; no LOW_PAYOUT flag. 2019 ($158.9M) and 2020 ($137.2M) were outlier-high years, likely driven by Harvard $200M disbursement schedule.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED (full 990-PF line-item grant recipients not parsed).

foundations_active.name: Blavatnik Family Foundation 2020
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (trustees Alexander Blavatnik and Sigmund D Heller).
foundations_active.structure_note: Appears to be a conduit/pass-through: takes large annual contributions, disburses most within same year, holds near-zero ending assets. Coexists with 81-2444350; not a successor. Reason for parallel structure not disclosed publicly.

foundations_active.name: Warner Music Group Blavatnik Family Foundation Social Justice Fund
foundations_active.attribution_note: Jointly funded by WMG (corporate) and BFF. Attribution to Blavatnik personal giving is partial. Grants modest (~$0.2M-$1.4M/year), below stated pace of the $100M/10-year 2020 pledge.

foundations_active.name: Blavatnik Archive Foundation
foundations_active.note: Separate entity focused on 20th-century Eastern European/Jewish history archives. Not parsed for grants-out here — listed for completeness.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Access Industries is Blavatnik's private industrial holding company, NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI. Used as a political-giving conduit, not a charitable vehicle.

uk_charitable_vehicles.name: Blavatnik Family Foundation (UK) and associated trusts
uk_charitable_vehicles.note: UK gifts to Oxford (£75M + £29M) and Tate Modern (£50M+) flowed through UK-domiciled vehicles not captured in US 990-PF data. Registered with UK Charity Commission; full filing detail NOT yet pulled.
uk_charitable_vehicles.todo: Pull UK Charity Commission registry records.

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: Access Industries historically maintained offshore holding structure (Mossack Fonseca / Pandora Papers-era press mentions Vekselberg and Fridman, not Blavatnik directly). ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

red_flags.evidence: Not a signatory to the Giving Pledge as of 2026-04. Despite $30B+ net worth and extensive named giving, Blavatnik has never publicly committed to giving away ≥50% of his wealth.

56 former US national security officials, academics, and activists wrote a public 2019 letter to the Council on Foreign Relations alleging Blavatnik's $12M CFR gift (and, by extension, gifts to Oxford, Harvard, Yale, Tate, Hudson Institute) were part of a longstanding campaign to 'launder his image in the West' and 'advance his access to political circles' — citing his 1997-2013 TNK-BP partnership with Viktor Vekselberg (US-sanctioned 2018) and Mikhail Fridman, and the $7B personal proceeds from the 2013 sale of the TNK-BP stake to Rosneft (run by Putin ally Igor Sechin). CFR president Richard Haass refused to return the gift. Blavatnik himself has NOT been sanctioned and faces no direct US allegation of corruption or election interference. DOCUMENTED WITHOUT JUDGMENT — the relevant academic/press sources are cited; classification is left to the reader.

$10M+ aggregate state+federal political contributions across Blavatnik, his wife Emily, and Access Industries (2015-2022). Includes $1M direct to Trump 2017 inaugural committee via Access Industries. Political giving of this scale typically displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33/$1 per NBER W26616.

2020 $100M/10-year pledge (WMG + BFF) has disbursed only ~$3.6M through 2025 via the dedicated 501(c)(3) (EIN 85-3060424). Even if additional WMG-corporate pass-throughs exist off-balance-sheet, the documented pace is materially behind the stated trajectory.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (BFF 2024 payout 77% of assets; 2019-2020 well above norm; all well above 5% minimum)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (all observable Blavatnik foundations show zero officer compensation)
PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED_GIVING_PLEDGE (he never signed — this is a separate NO_GIVING_PLEDGE flag)
DAF_TRANSFERS (not confirmed absent; full 990-PF recipient lists not parsed)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(BFF 81-2444350 grants_out 2017-2024) + sum(BFF 2020 85-1345780 grants_out 2021-2022) + sum(WMG SJF 85-3060424 grants_out 2020-2025) + pre-2017 direct gifts NOT captured in 990 window (Oxford 2010 $117.2M; Tate 2011 $80M; Harvard 2013 $50M; Yale 2013 $10M; TAU 2008 $5M; TAU 2014 $20M). Excludes: events already inside 990 window (Harvard 2018 $200M, Yale 2023 $40M, TAU 2018 $16M, CFR 2019 $12M, Harvard 2025 $18.86M — these are subsets of already-counted BFF grant_out totals). Excludes political giving and WMG SJF attribution-adjustment.

observable_giving_breakdown.adjustment_double_count_uk_to_us_bridge_: If pre-2017 Harvard 2013 $50M or Yale 2013 $10M flowed through US vehicles we haven't identified, minor double-count possible. We've deliberately left them ADDITIVE given pre-2017 BFF 81-2444350 did not exist. Revised sum: $1.018B observable. HOWEVER: we apply a conservative $205M haircut for (a) possible pre-2017 vehicles that eventually rolled forward into BFF opening balance, and (b) recognition that some of the 2020 BFF-2020 grantee payments may have been restricted-purpose transfers from BFF rather than Blavatnik-new money. Final observable: ~$813M.

Investigate relationship between BFF (81-2444350) and BFF 2020 (85-1345780): is the 2020 entity receiving contributions from the 2017 entity (internal transfer) or from Blavatnik directly? If internal transfer, subtract from observable to avoid double-counting.
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 for WMG (Blavatnik filings as beneficial owner) — check for stock gifts.
Run FEC bulk query for full Blavatnik/Access Industries political total through 2026.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.45 × $29.9B = $13.455B; tenure cap saturated (~20 years as billionaire). Expected = $1.3455B.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked via ICIJ. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.uk_giving_uplift_note: UK-domiciled gifts beyond the already-counted Oxford (2010) and Tate (2011) are plausible but unquantified: Royal Opera House, V&A, Hermitage (Blavatnik is a noted major UK arts donor per The Guardian 2022). Cap at $150M upper bound pending UK Charity Commission pull.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. 0.2% of liquid wealth = $27M baseline; Blavatnik shows a documented preference for named giving (almost every major gift comes with a named building/school/institute), so the anonymous-channel uplift is LOW. Cap at $60M.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Blavatnik is a major donor to Jewish causes (Tel Aviv University, 92nd Street Y, Jewish Federation-type grantees). Portion of this is already captured in 990-PF lines; religious-specific tithing norms add marginal uplift. Cap $30M.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: No public disclosure of a charitable trust holding Blavatnik's wealth (he has not signed a Giving Pledge and makes no equivalent public commitment). Zero.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Blavatnik is at ~0.78 — well above that threshold. Observable $813M against a $1.35B 10%-of-liquid expectation is ~60%, substantial named giving. He is NOT a 'low giver' in absolute or proportional terms. However, he is NOT a Tier E/F high-giver either: no Giving Pledge, no 95% commitment, no disclosed charitable trust, and the REPUTATION_LAUNDERING allegation (documented in CFR/Bellingcat/CNN) is a structural question mark independent of dollar totals. Classification: Tier C — documented substantial giving with documented governance concerns.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Blavatnik as the canonical example of 'significant named giving to elite institutions (Oxford, Harvard, Yale, Tate, TAU) concurrent with wealth sourced from post-Soviet aluminum/oil privatizations and a $7B TNK-BP sale to Rosneft.' The scale of giving ($800M+ observable) is real; the reputation-laundering allegation is documented in credible press and cited here without Scrooge adjudicating it. Not a Scrooge, not a saint — a legitimate data point readers can interpret.
